Short_Description: The fastest shipping for online shopping
Long_Description: 99minutos idea came when 2 friends and I went to a hookah bar and have some beers. When we got there they told us they ran out of charcoal for the hookah, so we said “We came here for that, is there anywhere we can order and receive it now?”, that is when we realized that the concept of fast delivery for e-commerce didn’t exist not only in mexico but in the world. So next day we launched an e-commerce store called 99minutos where anything you bought there got delivered in less than 99 minutes within mexico city. A couples of months later my 2 friends left the company so i remained as solo founder and the pivot the company, got rid of the e-commerce and focused only in the delivery.

Location: São Paulo, State of São Paulo, Brazil
Short_Description: Bare-metal access to Brazil's Open Finance and Pix systems
Long_Description: We're Daniel, Bruno, and Pedro — founders of Cumbuca. We’re the first proxy for Brazil’s regulated ecosystem. After years of building fintechs in Brazil, we saw how painful it is to obtain a license or depend on PSPs and BaaS providers. Our previous B2C operation scaled to over a million accounts, and we suffered since a lot of our core product relied on partner infra, and it hurt us to not be able to solve the issues affecting our customers. We’ve been deeply involved with Open Finance since its earliest days — we were one of the first players in the ecosystem and became the fourth ITP/PISP license ever issued in Brazil, and after closing our B2C operation decided to pivot to B2B and build the financial infrastructure partner we always wished existed for ourselves.

Short_Description: AI solution that streamlines candidate screening and recruitment
Long_Description: The driving force behind HoyTrabajas comes from a personal fire that's also burning in my co-founders. We saw how the traditional job boards have been a real headache for job seekers, especially those who are just starting. I know this because I've been there myself, hunting for my first job and not getting any feedback on my applications. That made me crazy at that moment. Now picture what's going down for all those people who aren't chasing professional jobs. They're probably in a rush to put food on the table for their families. And you know what? This struggle is very real for around 100 million low-skilled individuals in LATAM. People are feeling the pain of job finding. That was our motivation to be over this bote.
